verses.text: The word against Egypt opens with the call to battle: order the shields, draw near. The nations are summoned. God directs the affairs of empires as surely as those of His own people.
The word against Egypt opens with the call to battle: order the shields, draw near. The nations are summoned. God directs the affairs of empires as surely as those of His own people.
The word against Egypt opens with the call to battle: order the shields, draw near. The nations are summoned. God directs the affairs of empires as surely as those of His own people.
Though they harness their horses and arm for war, the mighty shall flee and stumble and fall. Their strength fails. No preparation avails against the purpose of God.
Though they harness their horses and arm for war, the mighty shall flee and stumble and fall. Their strength fails. No preparation avails against the purpose of God.
Though they harness their horses and arm for war, the mighty shall flee and stumble and fall. Their strength fails. No preparation avails against the purpose of God.
Who is this that rises like the Nile in flood? It is 'the day of the Lord GOD of hosts, a day of vengeance.' Proud Egypt is judged. The Lord of hosts repays the nations in His appointed day.
Who is this that rises like the Nile in flood? It is 'the day of the Lord GOD of hosts, a day of vengeance.' Proud Egypt is judged. The Lord of hosts repays the nations in His appointed day.

'Go up into Gilead, and take balm, O virgin... of Egypt: in vain shalt thou use many medicines.' No cure can be found. When God wounds a nation for its sin, human remedies are useless.
'Go up into Gilead, and take balm, O virgin... of Egypt: in vain shalt thou use many medicines.' No cure can be found. When God wounds a nation for its sin, human remedies are useless.

Nebuchadnezzar shall smite Egypt; their hired soldiers flee, and men cry, 'Pharaoh... is but a noise; he hath passed the time appointed.' Empty boasting collapses. Worldly power is loud until the hour of testing.

As surely as Tabor and Carmel stand, the foe will come; let Egypt furnish herself for captivity. The doom is fixed. God's word is as certain as the everlasting hills.
As surely as Tabor and Carmel stand, the foe will come; let Egypt furnish herself for captivity. The doom is fixed. God's word is as certain as the everlasting hills.
Egypt is a fair heifer, but destruction comes from the north; even her hired men flee like fattened bullocks. Her beauty cannot save. Outward splendour is no shield against God's appointed judgment.

God will punish the gods, the king, and the people of Egypt, and give them to Babylon; yet afterward it shall be inhabited again. Judgment is measured. Even in wrath God remembers mercy and a future.
God will punish the gods, the king, and the people of Egypt, and give them to Babylon; yet afterward it shall be inhabited again. Judgment is measured. Even in wrath God remembers mercy and a future.

'But fear not... O Jacob my servant'; He will save them from afar and make 'a full end of all the nations,' but only correct His own in measure. He distinguishes His people. God ends the wicked but chastens His children gently.
